---
title: "LCSM-082: Blending ↔ word reading — which direction dominates? (exploratory reciprocal coupling)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LCSM-082 is the **exploratory companion** of the time-lagged suite (#250): the
cross-lagged dominance question the wave-unrolled DAG makes first-class. Does a
child's prior-wave **word reading** predict their subsequent **blending**
change more strongly than prior blending predicts reading change? The forward
direction (phonological awareness → decoding) is the standard developmental
account; the reverse (reading experience sharpening phonological awareness) is
well-attested in typical development. The lagged DAG carries both
(`PA → WR` within-wave, `WR → PA` lagged), so which dominates is an empirical
question.

## Model structure

The generalised LCSM scaffold with processes $\{W, B, L\}$ and **reciprocal**
couplings — prior $B$ into the $W$ change and prior $W$ into the $B$ change —
plus letter-sounds ($L$), the one cheap measured confounder both directions
share, coupling into both. Both target equations carry the arm × window change
intercepts and the shared `hs` / `erbto` / `deapp_c` adjuster block; the $L$
equation stays bare.

The headline readout is the **SD-standardised dominance contrast**: per
posterior draw each coupling is standardised by the model's own latent scales
($g^{*} = g \cdot \mathrm{sd}(x_{\text{source}}) / \mathrm{sd}(\Delta_{\text{target}})$),
making the two directions comparable, and
$|g^{*}_{W \to B}| - |g^{*}_{B \to W}|$ is reported with its posterior
probability.

**Reading rule — stronger caveat than usual.** Unlike LCSM-081, whose
conditioning set equals a verified sufficient measured backdoor set, the
verified minimal set for *either* direction here needs roughly thirteen nodes,
including floored nonword reading and reading two waves back
(`notes/202607141030-time-lagged-model-designs.md`) — far beyond what is
fittable at this sample size. Both couplings are therefore **broadly-confounded
adjusted associations, in both directions**, and the dominance contrast is a
hypothesis-generator for a future cohort, not an evidence claim. The mild late
blending ceiling (14–21 % at 10/10 by t3–t4) is absorbed by the Beta-Binomial.

{{< include _partials/_results_lcsm.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
